senses.definition: stylized or exaggerated in a way that resembles cartoon drawing
examples: The poster had a cartoony look with bright colors and bold outlines.
Her expression was cartoonish, almost like a character from a comic strip.

senses.definition: the top or upper surface of a cart or wagon; a handle or roof area on a cart, sometimes used as a platform
examples: He stood on the cartop to tie the load.
The cartop was damaged in the storm.

senses.definition: a person who loves maps, cartography, or cartography-related items
examples: She is a true cartophile and collects old atlas editions.
Cartophiles often trade vintage maps at fairs.

senses.definition: plural of cartophile; people who love maps or maps collecting
examples: The convention attracted many cartophiles from around the world.
Cartophiles trade rare stamps and maps.

senses.definition: relating to cartophiles or to maps and cartography in a collecting context
examples: A cartophilic interest led him to acquire rare atlases.
Her cartophilic hobbies included restoring old maps.

senses.definition: plural of cartophily; the love of maps or map collecting
examples: Her cartophilies distracted her from other hobbies.
Cartophilies are often shared at map conventions.

senses.definition: a person who loves maps or map collecting; a cartophile
examples: A well-known cartophilist spoke at the atlas exhibition.
Cartophilists often bring rare maps to shows.

senses.definition: plural of cartophilist; people who love maps or map collecting
examples: Cartophilists gathered for the map fair.
Several renowned cartophilists donated antique maps.

senses.definition: the love of maps or map collecting; the hobby of cartophily
examples: His cartophily drove him to search for obscure maps.
Cartophily is a niche but passionate hobby.

senses.definition: a person who tops or caps a cart; an archaic or dialectal term related to carting
examples: The old guild had a cartopper who sealed shipments.

senses.definition: plural of cartopper; people who top or seal carts, historically
examples: Cartoppers ensured the crates were secure.

senses.definition: a carved or engraved device or protective enclosure; a cartridge-like insignia or design in heraldry
examples: The old coin bore a cartouch along its edge.
In architecture, a cartouch surround framed the inscription.

senses.definition: an oval or oblong framed inscription or emblem, often with ornate scrollwork; a carved enclosing tablet or decorative frame
examples: The cartouche around the royal name was elaborately decorated.
Ancient hieroglyphs were sometimes found within a cartouche.

senses.definition: plural of cartouche; decorative frames or inscriptions
examples: The museum displayed several cartouches from ancient monuments.
Intricate cartouches adorned the manuscript margins.

senses.definition: a container holding a substance (like ink, toner, or gunpowder) for a device or weapon; a unit in a cartridge-based product
examples: ink cartridge
bullets in a cartridge
printer cartridge

senses.definition: plural of cartridge
examples: We need new printer cartridges.
Several ammunition cartridges were stored safely.

senses.definition: a road intended for carts; a cart road
examples: The village relied on the old cartroad for freight.
Resting along the cartroad, they paused to read a sign.

senses.definition: plural of cartroad; roads used by carts
examples: The old carts moved slowly along the cartroads.
Cartroads connected rural settlements.

senses.definition: small vehicles with wheels used for transporting goods or people
examples: The supermarket has shopping carts.
They pushed the carts to the loading dock.

senses.definition: a sale of carts or a market event where carts are sold; a cart sale
examples: The town held a cartsale to raise funds.
We visited the cartsale to buy a second-hand cart.

senses.definition: plural of cartulary; medieval or historical registers or manuscripts containing deeds and titles
examples: The monastery housed several cartularies.
Scholars studied the cartularies for property records.

senses.definition: a medieval or historical register of charters and property deeds
examples: The cartulary listed all land grants.
Archivists preserved the cartulary as a valuable document.

senses.definition: containers or ware used for cart-based transport or packaging; cart-related goods
examples: The shop sold decorative cartware used for display.

senses.definition: a road or passage used by carts; a cart path
examples: The old cartway connected two villages.
They followed the cartway along the hillside.

senses.definition: plural of cartway; roads or paths for carts
examples: Several cartways were used by farmers.
The map shows multiple cartways crossing the valley.
